MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT LEXINGTON INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Appearances: James P. Wagoner of McCormick Barstow LLP (559.433.1300) for cross-complainant and moving party; Richard T. Collins of Berger Kahn (949.474.1880) for plaintiff and responding party.
Discussed and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Denied without prejudice to being re-filed on proper notice and in the proper form if the trial date is continued. The motion, along with the four other motions for summary adjudication, violates California Rule of Court 3.1113(d) limiting the number of pages of a memorandum filed in support of summary adjudication motion absent court order. The five separately filed motions are, in actuality, a single motion with separate issues that are, to a large extent, based on the same or similar set of facts. Even if the court did not have significantly reduced resources due to recent funding cutbacks, the court does not have the ability to read so many pages (many of which are duplicative or near-duplicative) for a single motion in a single case. Moreover, a cursory review of the motions disclose that the issues are neither so complex nor novel that they could not be adequately addressed within the applicable page limits or at least many fewer pages than comprise the five moving memoranda. Therefore, the court exercises its discretion not to consider the moving memoranda for any of the motions and thus denies the motions. (CRC 3.1113(g) (A memorandum exceeding the page limits is treated like a late-filed paper); Hobson v. Raychem Corporation (1999) 73 Cal. App. 4th 614, 623 (a court has the discretion to refuse to consider late-filed papers); CRC 3.113(a) (a moving memorandum is required for a summary adjudication motion). The parties may stipulate to a briefing schedule and submit the stipulation with a proposed order to the court by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
